creator: simon@FreeBSD.org
text:
On 2006.08.24 11:25:59 +0200, Henrik Brix Andersen wrote:
> 
> >Description:
> security/portaudit fails to overwrite the auditfile.tbz file when run
> as a non-root user since it sets a=r on the file once downloaded. The
> patch below solves this by mv'ing the file before attempting a new
> download instead of cp'ing it.
>
> >How-To-Repeat:
> Set $portaudit_dir in /usr/local/etc/portaudit.conf to a location
> writable by a non-root user and run 'portaudit -F' as that
> user. Notice that on the second run of 'portaudit -F' it fails to
> overwrite the auditfile.tbz.

Sorry about the delay.

I tried to reproduce this, but I can't.  It just works for me:

[simon@zaphod:~] tail -n 1 /usr/local/etc/portaudit.conf
portaudit_dir="/home/simon/portaudit"
[simon@zaphod:~] portaudit -F
New database installed.
[simon@zaphod:~] portaudit -F
New database installed.
[simon@zaphod:~] sudo sh -c 'echo portaudit_fetch_cmd=\"fetch -1ap\" >> /usr/local/etc/portaudit.conf'
[simon@zaphod:~] portaudit -F
auditfile.tbz                                 100% of   37 kB   48 kBps
New database installed.
[simon@zaphod:~] portaudit -F
auditfile.tbz                                 100% of   37 kB   48 kBps
New database installed.

The override of portaudit_fetch_cmd is to remove miror mode so fetch
always download even though there isn't a new version.  I see this
both on a recent FreeBSD 7.0 and 6.1-RELEASE.

Which FreeBSD version do you use?

-- 
Simon L. Nielsen
